No Poverty.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus links to poverty eradication or related targets.

Zero Hunger.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus links to hunger, food security, or sustainable agriculture.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus links to health, well-being, or related medical/public health topics.

Quality Education.reason: The course provides tertiary education (Target 4.3) and equips undergraduate students with 'relevant skills' in financial data analysis for 'future career' (Target 4.4).
evidence: introduce undergraduate level students some well-established econometric and statistical techniques
useful for their future career in the industry or study related to finance

Gender Equality.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus links to gender equality or women's empowerment.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus links to water, sanitation, or hygiene.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus links to energy access, renewable energy, or energy efficiency.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course provides 'high-value skills' in financial data analysis for 'future career' (Target 8.5), enhancing economic productivity (Target 8.2) and strengthening financial institutions (Target 8.10).
evidence: econometric and statistical techniques for financial data analysis
extremely useful for their future career in the industry

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course enhances 'technological capabilities' through econometric techniques and R programming for financial analysis (Target 9.5), indirectly supporting access to financial services for enterprises (Target 9.3).
evidence: financial data analysis with R, a free and popular software for scientific data analysis
CAPM, factor models and their applications

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The analytical skills in financial data analysis are foundational for improving the 'regulation and monitoring of global financial markets' (Target 10.5), which can help reduce inequalities.
evidence: econometric and statistical techniques for financial data analysis
Linear regression models and an introduction to financial time series

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus links to urban development, housing, or community planning.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: The core competency 'To understand and identify...sustainability issues' combined with financial analysis skills can support integrating sustainability information into company reporting (Target 12.6).
evidence: To understand and identify ethical, socially responsible, and sustainability issues

Climate Action.reason: While 'sustainability issues' are mentioned, the course content lacks specific links to climate change mitigation, adaptation, or policy.

Life Below Water.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus links to marine ecosystems, ocean health, or fisheries.

Life on Land.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus links to terrestrial ecosystems, biodiversity, or deforestation.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: Financial data analysis skills can contribute to strengthening 'transparent institutions' (Target 16.6) and potentially identifying 'illicit financial flows' (Target 16.4).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course builds 'statistical capacity' using R for financial analysis (Target 17.19) and enhances tools for understanding 'macroeconomic stability' (Target 17.13).
evidence: statistical techniques for financial data analysis with R
support statistical capacity-building in developing countries
